title: quercus robusta
intro: Quercus robusta, also known as the Evergreen Oak, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, characterized by its broad, rounded crown and dark green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Quercus robusta include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Quercus robusta include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and capsules for medicinal purposes.
